---
title: "Hi, I'm Irene"
output:
  html_document:
    includes:
      in_header: cover.html
      css: float.css
---



Welcome to my personal website!


::: {style="display: flex;"}

::: {style="width: 50%;"}
<div>

<p align="justify">
I am a political scientist specialising in political behaviour and quantitative methods. I hold a PhD from [Universitat Pompeu Fabra](https://www.upf.edu/web/2019-2020/deppol), where I worked as a member of the VolkswagenStiftung-funded [COVIDEU Project](https://www.covideu.info).
</p>

<p align="justify">
I also hold a BA in Political Science and Public Administration by the [Universitat de Girona (UdG)](http://www.udg.edu), an MA in Political Analysis and Institutional Assessment by the [Universitat de Barcelona (UB)](https://www.ub.edu/web/portal/ca/) and a postgraduate degree in Data Analysis also by the UB. My research interests include political behavior and attitudes, far-right politics and quantitative methods.
</p>

<p align="justify">
My research combines inputs from political behaviour, political institutions, and political communication, with a strong methodological focus on quantitative research designs and causal inference
</p>

<p align="justify">
My dissertation, [Crises, Competition, and Campaigning: Citizens and Parties in 21st Century Politics](thesis.html), studied how disruptive events can influence and reshape political evaluations and strategic behaviour. Across three different papers, I use both observational and experimental data to identify how information shocks affect institutional support and how political actors adapt under changing incentives. You can check the research I am currently working on [here](papers.html).
</p>


</div>
:::

::: {style="width: 50%;"}
<div>
```{r me, echo=FALSE, fig.align='center', fig.dim=c(4,2), out.width = "60%", fig.cap = "© Cecilia Coca"}
knitr::include_graphics("001184--002--VK_1.jpg")
```


</div>
:::
:::
